welcome.title: Choose your language
welcome.subtitle: We save your choice in the browser. Menus, help and trust copy then load in that language — change anytime via the control in the bar above.
welcome.valueLead: The platform brings together market data, signals and controlled execution paths. Paper practises without real exchange orders; shadow often observes decisions in parallel with the live chain; live moves real capital only with approvals and policy gates. AI summarises and answers questions — it is not investment advice, does not place background orders, and does not replace your own risk management.
welcome.hintStorage: We need this so the interface can load reliably for you.
welcome.destOnboarding: short guided setup (account, demo, chart, AI, safety)
welcome.destConsole: console — overview with chart, AI and account

situationAiExplain.title: What this state means
situationAiExplain.lead: First, a factual summary from the diagnostic data we already have. Uncertainty is called out. Optionally, you can request a deeper model-assisted review below — it complements telemetry, it does not replace it.
situationAiExplain.uncertaintyBanner: At least one aspect is not clearly evidenced from the current snapshot — check technical details or health instead of guessing.

problem.healthSnapshotFailed: The central health snapshot could not be loaded: {detail}
problem.edgeBlocksReads: Edge/bootstrap diagnostics are blocking reads to core APIs — many console views may be empty or stale.
problem.componentsDegraded: There is no single cross-cutting incident, but these components are not OK: {list}
problem.noExplicitIncident: No additional cross-cutting incident is recorded; the situation is driven mainly by component rows and the summary above.

technical.uncertain: The technical root cause cannot be determined unambiguously from this snapshot — inspect the relevant technical folds, logs, or health details.
technical.openTechnicalFold: The short version is in the message fields above — see the technical fold for detail.

why.fallbackDegraded: While parts of the chain are impaired, market view, signals, or execution paths are not reliable enough for operational decisions.
why.dataOrView: Without trustworthy data or connectivity, statements in this view are not reliable.
why.surfaceTrust: Without trustworthy market data or transport, the chart and side panels are not trustworthy — do not rely on this view alone for decisions.

areas.unknown: Affected areas are not clearly delimited from the snapshot — review health and the component list.
areas.surface: This surface ({id}) and connected live/chart paths.

appTried.noneKnown: No automatic remediation attempts or background jobs are visible in the snapshot — some may run outside this dashboard.
appTried.noneStated: The message does not describe an explicit platform action.
appTried.surfaceClient: The dashboard can refresh locally, reconnect streams, and show hints — backend fixes run on workers/gateway.

next.fallback: Open health, diagnostics, and live terminal; compare timestamps and affected services.
next.reloadOrHealth: Reload the page; if it persists, open health and diagnostics.
next.surface: Check symbol/timeframe, open live terminal with the same context, read the data-flow panel, and escalate to ops with a timestamp if needed.

selfHeal.manualRequired: At least one item expects a manual step (configuration, approval, or operations) — do not assume full automation will clear it.
selfHeal.partialAutomatic: There is documented automation (recheck hints, partial client refresh). A manual step may still be required until the cause is gone.
selfHeal.observeAndEscalate: From the data shown, it is unclear whether automation will help — observe, measure, and escalate if it persists.
selfHeal.retriesMayRun: The platform may use retries or queues — that does not fix every failure but reduces transient errors.
selfHeal.platformReported: According to the message, the platform is doing: {snippet}
selfHeal.noAutoFixAssumed: No reliable self-heal can be inferred from the message — manual verification is appropriate.
selfHeal.surfaceMostlyManual: The client can only refresh and reconnect; data gaps are fixed upstream — operations intervention is often required.

situationAiExplain.llmFoldLead: Sends the same facts to the governed operator-explain backend. The answer is advisory and has no order or configuration authority.
situationAiExplain.llmSkippedLowSeverity: For pure info messages we skip deeper model review — the summary above is enough.
situationAiExplain.llmQuestion: Du bist ein vorsichtiger Betriebs-Analyst für eine Trading-Plattform. Im JSON-Feld readonly_context_json liegt ein Paket kind=situation_explain_v1 oder ähnlich mit Inzidenten, Komponenten und einer bereits erzeugten deterministic_summary (sieben Felder). Aufgaben: (1) Bestätige oder korrigiere die Einordnung nur mit Bezug zu diesen Fakten — nichts erfinden. (2) Wenn Ursachen unklar sind, sage das klar. (3) Strukturiere deine Antwort in explanation_de als gut lesbaren Fließtext mit kurzen Absätzen: erst operative Kurzfassung, dann technische Details, dann Risiko, dann konkrete nächste Checks. (4) referenced_artifacts_de: bis zu 8 kurze Stichpunkte, welche Runbooks oder Bereiche relevant sein könnten (ohne erfundene Dateinamen). (5) non_authoritative_note_de: ein Satz, dass dies keine Freigabe für Live-Handel ist. Antworte auf Deutsch.
situationAiExplain.llmQuestionEn: You are a cautious operations analyst for a trading platform. The JSON field readonly_context_json contains a package kind=situation_explain_v1 (or similar) with incidents, components, and a deterministic_summary (seven fields). Tasks: (1) Confirm or correct the assessment using only these facts — do not invent details. (2) If root causes are unclear, say so plainly. (3) Structure explanation_de as readable paragraphs: operational summary first, then technical detail, then risk, then concrete next checks. (4) referenced_artifacts_de: up to 8 short bullets on relevant runbook areas (no fabricated file names). (5) non_authoritative_note_de: one sentence that this is not approval for live trading. Write explanation_de in German.

expectation.loading: Fetching data — you will shortly see whether connectivity looks good and how fresh values are.
expectation.fetchFailed: This area could not load its data. Other pages may still work — check connectivity or the gateway.
expectation.noLive: No fresh live market data here. Charts and signals may be empty or incomplete until the pipeline recovers.
expectation.stale: Market data is older than expected. You still see values, but treat decisions with extra care.
expectation.degradedRead: The server returned a limited view — some fields may be missing or older than usual.
expectation.partialGeneric: Not every data segment is complete. Review gaps and affected areas above.
expectation.partialSignals: The signal list may be empty, filtered, or the API may report a degraded read — this is usually not a browser issue.
expectation.liveDemo: Demo or test data is active. The UI does not claim a full live exchange path.
expectation.execLanePaper: Market data may be current; execution is paper — no real-money orders go out on this configuration.
expectation.execLaneShadow: Market data may be current; shadow observes or mirrors — check live approvals separately.
expectation.liveLineageGaps: Candles may show, but the pipeline only has data on {ok} of {total} segments — some extras may be missing.
expectation.liveOk: For this view, core market data looks plausibly fresh. Still verify approvals and risk as you normally would.
expectation.unknown: System state is ambiguous — use Health, live terminal, or support if something important depends on it.

healthSnapshot.title: System health unreachable
healthSnapshot.body: Without health, execution lane and some context on this page are missing. Candles and other APIs may still respond independently.
healthSnapshot.step1: Reload or wait briefly (transient network issues).
healthSnapshot.step2: Open system health and connectivity diagnostics in the console.
healthSnapshot.step3: Check the self-healing hub for maintenance or configuration notices.

backgroundRefresh.title: Showing cached data — refresh failed
backgroundRefresh.body: The UI may be based on an older server response. Once connectivity is stable, a reload usually brings the latest data.
backgroundRefresh.step2: Check edge status or gateway reachability.
backgroundRefresh.step3: If it persists: use diagnostic mode (?diagnostic=1) and logs.

console.sse: Real-time push from the server (Server-Sent Events). Alternative: periodic HTTP refresh.
console.polling: Data is fetched on a fixed HTTP interval — no standing push channel.
console.ingest: Ingestion: raw market data is processed into history/buffers.
console.ood: Unfamiliar market context for the model (out-of-distribution) — interpret cautiously.
console.maeBps: MAE: possible adverse price path vs. the position, in basis points.
console.mfeBps: MFE: possible favourable price path, in basis points.
console.expectedReturnBps: Expected edge in basis points — indicative, not a profit guarantee.
console.facetSample: Sample from recent signals — not the full account universe.
console.metaDecision: Meta decision layer above the base signal.
console.thRiskGov: Risk governor: live clearance, blockers and probabilities.
console.thStopLev: Stop distances, executability and leverage hints.
console.thExecution: Last execution decision and mirror status.
console.thDecision: Direction, strength and system trade suggestion.

language.brief: Choose the interface language for menus and help texts.
language.detail: Your choice is stored as a cookie and persists after reload. Without it the app cannot load texts reliably. You can change language here or on the welcome screen.

mode.brief: Simple view shows fewer menu items; Pro shows every console area.
mode.detail: After onboarding you often start in simple view. Switch to Pro when you need market universe, learning, health, etc. The setting is stored in the browser.

usage.brief: Plan, consumption and AI usage — when billing runs through the platform.
usage.detail: All figures and caps are delivered securely from the server, not the browser. List USD is a list price. Monthly caps limit AI usage. If the page stays empty or errors, billing may not be active yet — common in test environments.
usage.emptyBody: Once your plan is provisioned, summary and ledger rows appear here. An empty list is normal when there is no usage in this period.
usage.step1: Wait a moment and reload the page.
usage.step2: Ask support or operations whether billing is enabled for your account.
usage.step3: Open again once your contract or trial is active.
usage.ledgerHint: No ledger rows yet — normal when there is no usage this period.

telegram.brief: Approvals show what waits for you; the Telegram column shows notification status.
telegram.detail: Telegram is a notification channel: you may see sent/failed and a message id. It does not replace your own risk review. No log files needed in the browser.

approvals.emptyBody: Nothing is waiting — often good, or the pipeline has not produced new items.
approvals.step1: Check the cockpit for incoming data.
approvals.step2: Review the live broker journal for executions.
approvals.step3: If you expect Telegram alerts, check approvals settings.

broker.brief: Live broker: orders, runtime and kill-switch on the server — no API keys in the browser.
broker.detail: Sensitive connections run securely on the platform. You see status, audit trail and metadata. Real trading actions require valid sign-in and approvals.
broker.runtimeEmptyBody: The server has not returned a current status yet — e.g. after a restart or a broken connection.
broker.runtimeStep1: Check with operations that the platform and broker connectivity are running.
broker.runtimeStep2: Reload the page after a few minutes.
broker.runtimeUnavailable: Connection status could not be loaded — see the notice above.

risk.brief: Cockpit: market posture, signal, key figures and warnings at a glance.
risk.detail: No single number replaces your risk management. Colors show posture and constraints; details live under signals, broker and health.

signals.pageBrief: Signals are system assessments — not automatic buy orders. Filters help you search.
signals.pageDetail: Columns show direction, decision, leverage hints and Telegram status. 'Live clear' does not mean guaranteed profit — approvals are separate.
signals.emptyBody: Nothing matches or the pipeline has not delivered data for this scope yet.
signals.step2: Check the cockpit for incoming data.
signals.step3: Use the live terminal to verify candles and data path.

drift.brief: Drift: how model posture and online learning change over time.
drift.detail: Levels like warn or block are server-side safeguards, not marketing. Registry and reports add context.

monitor.brief: Service health, alerts and delivery queue — operations, not marketing uptime.
monitor.detail: Yellow or red: check which service is affected and take the next operational step. No browser logs required.

liveTerminal.brief: Live chart and data origin — are prices current or is a segment missing?
liveTerminal.detail: Top row: connection and freshness in plain language. “Where does market data come from?” lists each step from the feed to the chart. “Pause” only freezes the view, not the server. Technical detail: add ?diagnostic=1 to the URL.

liveEmpty.title: Chart without candles?
liveEmpty.body: Fresh market data may be missing for this symbol and interval — or the platform has not delivered a full path yet.
liveEmpty.s1: Open “Where does market data come from?” and read any “missing” rows.
liveEmpty.s2: Use “Refresh data” or reload; try another symbol or a shorter interval.
liveEmpty.s3: Open Ops posture or system status; contact operations if it persists.

depositPublic.brief: Top-ups and plans run through the platform — securely on the server, not in the browser.
depositPublic.detail: The product page explains transparency. Payments are processed safely on the server side.

telegramPublic.brief: Telegram can send approval hints — optional and under your control.
telegramPublic.detail: No sensitive setup in the browser; status appears under approvals and in signal details.

welcome.brief: Pick a language first — then continue to the app or the short orientation.
welcome.detail: Language drives all UI text and help. Change it later in the top bar. Without a cookie the shell cannot resolve the UI. Under the heading you can read calmly what paper, shadow and live mean so the next steps stay clear.

onboarding.brief: A few steps to account, practice, first chart, AI help and clear boundaries.
onboarding.detail: Nothing is mandatory — links open the right areas. Skip goes to the console. Paper = practice without real orders; shadow = parallel observation of the decision chain; live = real capital only with approvals and gates. AI explains; it does not decide your risk for you.

healthPage.brief: Service status, alerts and the language assistant — situational awareness, not silent actions.
healthPage.detail: See whether data paths and workers are reachable and which monitor alerts are open. The assistant answers structured questions on the server; there is no long-lived chat here, no automatic orders and no strategy changes from the dialogue alone. Paper stays simulation; shadow often compares signals/decisions with live; only the approved live path moves real money.

paperPage.brief: Paper is practice: demo account and simulator — no real exchange orders.
paperPage.detail: Figures come from the platform. The equity curve reflects closed practice trades; open positions are marked. Live orders and real money are separate (live broker / approvals). Shadow compares paths in other console views — here you see the practice lane only. Empty tables usually mean missing data or no demo account yet — not necessarily a mistake on your side.

consoleHelpHub.brief: Quick paths to chart, practice, AI questions and account — without hunting the menu.
consoleHelpHub.detail: Use the list like a table of contents. Paper stays simulation; signals and AI add transparency, not buy advice; system & status shows whether the platform delivers data, plus the assistant. Pro areas remain behind “All features (Pro)”.

selfHealingPage.brief: Diagnostics hub with full inventory, prioritized incidents, and server-side re-measurement.
selfHealingPage.detail: Self Healing aggregates edge bootstrap, system health, monitor alerts, and the outbox. Automatic steps are limited to safe re-probing and UI refresh—worker restarts and secrets stay with ops and deploy.

consoleHelp.title: Help & overview
consoleHelp.subtitle: Orientation instead of searching: where to go for market view, practice, AI and account — without opening the full Pro menu.
consoleHelp.intro: Each link opens a finished area; short help (?) appears where it matters. Paper practises without real money; signals and AI add transparency, not buy advice; system & status shows reachability and the question assistant. Pro areas (cockpit, broker, learning, …) sit behind “All features (Pro)”.

signals.title: Signal center
signals.subtitle: Assessments, filters and transparency — not buy advice.

filters.facetsMirrorNote: Counts reflect what appeared in recent signals — not a promise of the full catalog. Values appear once they truly occur in this environment.
filters.hintStrategyName: Strategy name in DB — API: strategy_name
filters.hintFacetCounts: Distinct values in the sample — see footnote
filters.hintPlaybookIdExact: Exact playbook id for the URL — API: playbook_id
filters.facetsWindowNote: Sample: last {rows} signals by time (distinct values). Not the full account universe — observed history only.

broker.title: Live broker
broker.subtitle: Orders, runtime, kill-switch and audit — read securely from the server.
broker.liveLaneReadyNoExtra: No extra safety notes in this snapshot — if shadow-match is enabled, see the list above.
broker.killSwitchInactiveExplain: No active kill-switch. That alone does not block live — check the “Live order path” banner, latch, config and reconcile.

liveLane.live_lane_unknown: Unclear — reconcile data missing or empty

broker.partialLoadTitle: Some live-broker sections could not be loaded
broker.partialLoadBody: Other cards show valid data, empty lists or gateway hints — try again later or contact operations.
broker.sectionUnavailable: This section could not be loaded — see the list above.
broker.tableEmptyOperational: No rows in the current window — normal when there is no activity.
broker.bitgetNoDiagnostic: No Bitget private diagnostics in the last reconcile — live-broker must run and write reconcile, or live is not wired in this setup.
broker.orderStatusEmpty: No order status counts in the snapshot — common before the first order or before the worker published state.
broker.instrumentEmpty: No instrument metadata in the last reconcile.
broker.reconcileEmpty: No reconcile details in the snapshot.

learning.title: Learning & drift
learning.subtitle: Model posture, online drift and registry — numbers from the server you can trace.
learning.loadDataPrefix: We could not load the data:
learning.modelOpsErrFallback: We could not load the model-ops report.
learning.onlineDriftLead: How far current model posture diverges from what we allow for live trading. Warning, throttled live or hard block are enforced on the server as safeguards. See operations docs for online drift; flag names live in server configuration.
learning.noDriftState: No row found — is migration 400 missing?
learning.registryLead: The registry defines which model runs as champion and which challengers may load — optionally scoped by cluster or symbol. Writes are protected technically; this page is read-only. Enablement and calibration requirements are in server configuration.
learning.registryEmpty: No rows — registry migration or seed may be missing.
learning.modelOpsLead: Compact report on calibration, recent drift events and signal slices — from the gateway (`GET /v1/learning/model-ops/report`).

health.title: System & status
health.subtitle: Service health, open alerts, integrations and optionally the language assistant — so you know whether the platform is delivering data and where to ask next. The assistant explains; it does not grant trade clearance or change your setup silently.
health.loadErrorHint: We could not load the status right now. Wait a moment and reload the page. If it keeps happening, make sure the full application stack is running (see the README quickstart, e.g. pnpm dev:up). For operators: turn on diagnostic mode in the menu or run pnpm local:doctor in the project folder to see whether the gateway, sign-in, or network is the cause.
health.matrixLead: Broker, Telegram, payments, LLM, monitoring, and gateway — feature flags, health, rolling timestamps (migration 602).
health.matrixUnavailable: Integrations matrix unavailable (gateway or DB issue).
health.alertsLead: Open technical notices — for ops and runbooks, not a vanity uptime score.
health.pdfDownloadHint: Warnings, machine blocks, services, Redis, integrations matrix, alerts and outbox — same substance as the health API, for tickets and AI workflows.
health.machineDetailsToggle: Technical details (JSON) — for scripts & AI; collapsed by default
health.connectivityTitle: Likely root cause before data warnings: services unreachable
health.connectivityLead: Many “Services” rows showing ~2s timeouts or connection refused mean the API gateway cannot reach workers (Docker network, HEALTH_URL_*, or services not running). Stale candles/signals/news warnings are usually a consequence — not the root fix.
health.connectivityB1: On the host: docker compose ps — market-stream, signal-engine, feature-engine, structure-engine, drawing-engine, paper-broker, monitor-engine, alert-engine should be up.
health.connectivityB2: Gateway env: point HEALTH_URL_* / internal URLs at Docker service names (e.g. http://market-stream:PORT), not localhost from the wrong network namespace.
health.connectivityB3: Windows/WSL/firewall: ensure the gateway container can reach worker hosts/ports; fix mass timeouts before chasing individual freshness alerts.
health.connectivityB4: ops.alerts: after the pipeline is stable, review stale open rows; locally use pnpm alerts:close-local or scripts/sql/ after triage.
health.connectivityExtraMonitorRefused: monitor-engine: connection refused (~1 ms) — nothing is listening on the target (service not running, wrong port, or HEALTH_URL_MONITOR_ENGINE). Check `docker compose ps monitor-engine` and logs; without monitor-engine many SQL-SLO rows in ops.alerts can remain open.
health.connectivityExtraSplitBrain: Pattern: some services (e.g. news-engine, llm-orchestrator) are ok while others fail after ~2s — usually inconsistent or missing HEALTH_URL_* per worker; compare each URL with the working ones in the gateway env. Redis may still show old stream lengths; a fresh pipeline needs reachable workers.
health.paperPathTitle: Path to signals and paper trading (AI pipeline)
health.paperPathLead: Order: reachable services and fresh candles first, then signals in Redis/DB, then automated execution — live money is a separate, stricter step.
health.paperStep1: Services green: gateway probes without timeout; Redis stream events:candle_close growing; ideally events:signal_created > 0.
health.paperStep2: market-stream + Bitget demo/network: fresh 1m candles; otherwise the signal chain stays empty.
health.paperStep3: strategy_execution_mode: set to auto for automated strategy-driven execution (policy permitting) and restart gateway; manual = intentionally no auto-fire.
health.paperStep4: Live orders: additionally live gate, approvals, exchange keys and execution_tier — never “flip” from UI alone.
health.readinessManualExplainer: Current: strategy_execution_mode is manual — the server will not automatically turn strategy signals into orders. For automated paper/shadow steps (if allowed), set STRATEGY_EXECUTION_MODE=auto in app/gateway settings and restart the gateway service.
health.cockpitCta: Open operator cockpit for posture and queues
health.execLinePaper: Paper mode: no real-money exchange path; practice runs through paper-broker once the gateway can reach it.
health.execLineManual: Strategy mode manual: the strategy will not auto-trigger orders.
health.execLineLiveSubmitOff: Live submission: off — no live orders are sent to the exchange.
health.aiExplainLead: Ask a concrete question in German. The service answers in a structured way via API gateway and LLM orchestrator — no order authority and no persistent chat history. Optionally attach a small JSON object as read-only context (e.g. snippets from health or signals).
health.aiExplainQuestionPlaceholder: e.g. What does “live gate” mean in this stack?
health.aiExplainContextHint: Leave empty or use compact JSON (object). Very large payloads are rejected — include only relevant fields.
health.aiExplainNote: Treat this as orientation only — not an order or a setting change. Double-check runbooks and your live configuration before acting.
health.aiExplainFakeBanner: Test provider (fake) is active: no external LLM was called. The answer is deterministic and meant for technical verification.
health.aiExplainErrQuestionShort: Enter at least 3 characters in the question field.
health.aiExplainErrContextJson: Context must be valid JSON (a single object).
health.aiExplainErrContextNotObject: Context JSON must be an object, not an array.
health.aiExplainErrQuestionLong: Question exceeds the maximum length (8000 characters).
health.aiExplainErrUpstreamNotOk: The model service reported ok=false — see logs or retry.
health.aiExplainTraceBody: These IDs match gateway and LLM-orchestrator logs (JSON fields corr_request_id / corr_correlation_id).
health.aiExplainErrAborted: Request cancelled. You can change the question and try again.
health.aiExplainErrTimeout: The request took too long and was stopped. Check that the LLM service is running, then try again.
health.aiExplainErrIncompleteResponse: The service returned data we could not interpret (missing explanation). Please try again or contact support if this persists.
health.aiExplainErrAuth: You are not signed in for this action, or your session does not allow gateway access. Refresh the page or check dashboard authentication settings.
health.aiExplainErrRateLimit: Too many requests in a short time. Please wait a moment and try again.
health.aiExplainErrOrchestratorConfig: The API gateway cannot reach the LLM orchestrator or internal credentials are missing. On the gateway, set INTERNAL_API_KEY and LLM_ORCH_BASE_URL (or HEALTH_URL_LLM_ORCHESTRATOR), then restart.
health.aiExplainErrBffAuth: The dashboard server is not configured to call the API gateway. Set DASHBOARD_GATEWAY_AUTHORIZATION (Bearer JWT with gateway:read) in the dashboard environment and restart the dev server.
health.aiExplainErrServiceUnavailable: The service is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
health.aiExplainErrOpenaiKey: The LLM orchestrator cannot call the model provider: OpenAI API key is missing or invalid. Set OPENAI_API_KEY on the orchestrator (or use the fake provider only for local tests).
health.aiExplainErrLlmUnavailable: The model service could not complete the request. Try again; if it keeps failing, check orchestrator logs and provider status.
health.aiExplainErrBadGateway: A server in the chain returned an error while calling the model. Try again shortly.
health.aiExplainErrServer: A server error occurred. Please try again later.
health.aiExplainErrGeneric: Something went wrong (HTTP {status}). Try again or check the health page.
health.aiExplainErrOffline: We could not reach the server. Check your network, VPN, or whether the API gateway is running.
health.aiExplainErrUnexpectedHtml: The server returned an unexpected page instead of data. Check gateway URL and proxy settings.
health.aiExplainErrPayloadTooLarge: The request or prompt was too large. Shorten the question or context and try again.
health.aiExplainErrContextTooLarge: The optional JSON context is too large. Trim to the essential fields and try again.
health.aiExplainErrCircuitOpen: The model service temporarily stopped calling the provider (circuit open) after repeated errors. Wait briefly and retry; if it persists, check orchestrator logs.
health.aiExplainErrProviderNotConfigured: No usable LLM provider is configured on the orchestrator (e.g. missing OpenAI key with fake disabled). Check environment or enable the fake provider for local tests.
health.aiExplainErrLlmRetryExhausted: The model service could not finish after several attempts (e.g. timeout or rate limit). Retry later or contact operations.
health.safetyDiagLead: Maps your question to a current snapshot of system health, open monitor alerts and the alert outbox. You get structured root-cause hints, affected services and next checks — as readable text, with nothing executed “in the background” because of it.
health.safetyDiagBoundary: Boundary: no silent live actions; no changes to order limits, broker settings, or payment-sensitive configuration. Proposed shell commands are text suggestions only — review before running and align with your runbooks.
health.safetyDiagQuestionPlaceholder: e.g. Why is the database red and which services should I check first?
health.safetyDiagContextHint: Default: filled from this page. You may trim fields or add excerpts from Terminal/Shadow views — do not paste secrets.
health.safetyDiagFakeBanner: Test provider (fake) active: no external LLM was called. The response is deterministic for technical verification.
health.safetyDiagEnvHints: Environment / config hints (names only, not values)
health.safetyDiagCommandsWarn: Do not run automatically — review manually, permissions, and production rules.
health.safetyDiagErrQuestionShort: Enter at least 3 characters in the question.
health.safetyDiagErrQuestionLong: The question exceeds the allowed length (max. 8000 characters).
health.safetyDiagErrIncomplete: The service returned data we could not interpret (missing summary). Please try again.
health.assistLayerLead: Four assistant roles exist in the product overall — here are the two for operations and market logic. Each tab is its own segment: separate transcript, separate context keys, and no mixing with account assist on the Account page.
health.assistSegmentScopeNote: Switching tabs starts a new conversation ID and clears the transcript so the UI matches server storage (Redis, keyed by assist role).
health.assistMessagePlaceholder: e.g. What does this alert mean in the platform context?
health.assistContextHintGeneric: Only allowed top-level keys (the gateway drops everything else).
health.assistContextHintAdminOps: Allowed keys include: platform_health, signals_summary, orders_snapshot, pnl_snapshot, alerts_open, contracts_summary, billing_platform_snapshot, help_page_excerpt, operator_notes.
health.assistContextHintStrategy: Allowed keys include: signal_snapshot, signal_history, orders_readonly, pnl_readonly, risk_limits, strategy_meta, help_page_excerpt.
health.assistErrEmptyReply: The reply had no text — please try again.
health.assistSeparationNote: No implicit approval: trades, limits, and configuration are not changed by this assistant. Replies are guidance inside the selected segment — not the same as operator explain or the one-off strategy-signal explanation.

grid.databaseOkHint: — connection and core schema OK (gaps show under data freshness).
grid.databaseBadHint: — technical issue (migrations or tables), not a normal empty state; see schema/migrations below.
grid.migrationCatalogNote: (migration catalog not mounted on gateway — comparing tables only)

integrations.title: Integration check
integrations.subtitle: Live verification from GET /v1/system/health: integrations matrix (logical groupings) and per-service HTTP /health probes. No secrets — only status, latency, and public error text.
integrations.summaryOkTitle: All integration rows are healthy or intentionally disabled.
integrations.summaryAttentionTitle: Some integrations need attention before relying on production data.
integrations.summaryCounts: OK {ok} · disabled {disabled} · degraded / not configured {degraded} · error / misconfigured {bad} — {total} rows total.
integrations.summaryNoDataBody: The gateway did not return a matrix (health failed or older build). Check edge status and gateway logs.
integrations.probesLead: Each row is a configured HEALTH_URL_* endpoint on the gateway. not_configured means the URL is empty — the service is not probed.
integrations.linkHealth: Open full health page (alerts, outbox, freshness)

ops.lead: Here you see market posture, risk limits and system state in one place. Anything that can move real money needs a valid server session.
ops.priceChartLead: Same symbol and timeframe as above. Zoom and pan with mouse or touch. Full live view:
ops.errHealth: We could not load the system overview.
ops.errLiveState: We could not load live market data for this symbol.

paper.title: Paper trading
paper.subtitle: Practice ledger ({symbol}) — no real exchange orders. All figures come from the platform.
paper.leadParagraph: You practise with demo capital and simulator logic. Shadow and live are separate: shadow compares paths in other console areas; live moves real money only with approvals. Empty tables usually mean no data yet or no demo account — not a personal failure.
paper.trustPanelTitle: Paper, shadow and live — in short
paper.trustPanelBody: Paper: practice trades and account simulation, no exchange orders. Shadow: often parallel assessment of the same or similar signals versus the live chain — useful for drift, not a second order book. Live: only when your tenant and platform approvals allow; orders run server-side, never with API keys in the browser.
paper.adminDetailsSummary: For operations & support: control demo account (API)
paper.partialLoadTitle: Some sections could not be loaded
paper.partialLoadBody: Other cards show the last good data or an empty state — try again later or contact operations.
paper.equityCurveExplain: Curve = demo account starting equity plus cumulative realised PnL from closed trades (learn.trade_evaluations). Without a demo account there is no curve.
paper.equityCurveNoAccount: No demo account yet — the equity curve appears after the account exists and trades close.
paper.noAccount: No demo account is set up yet. Please contact operations or support.
paper.ledgerLead: Deposits, withdrawals and admin markers — separate from position-bound fees.
paper.ledgerFallbackFromMetrics: The ledger API was not reachable. Showing a short excerpt from account metrics (fewer rows than the full list).
paper.journalLead: Chronological trail for learning/support; filtered by symbol when selected.
paper.journalEmpty: No journal entries in this window.
paper.adminHintBody: Mutations require admin:write: POST /v1/admin/paper/account/deposit-demo (positive/negative amount), POST /v1/admin/paper/account/adjust (delta), POST /v1/admin/paper/account/reset-demo (new starting equity, optional purge_trade_evaluations). Each change should be traceable in the gateway audit log — end users normally never see this.

shadowLive.title: Shadow vs. live
shadowLive.subtitle: Journal and paper window for {symbol} / {timeframe}. Identical outcomes only where flags and payloads show it — not implied from the same order book.
shadowLive.partialLoadBody: Other cards use what loaded or explain the gap — retry later or contact operations.
shadowLive.provenanceDecisions: Execution decisions: live.execution_decisions + payload_json.shadow_live_divergence (match_ok, hard/soft_violations) — gateway GET /v1/live-broker/decisions/recent.
shadowLive.provenanceFills: Live fills: exchange / live-broker — GET /v1/live-broker/fills/recent (window; no 1:1 paper mapping without execution_id).
shadowLive.provenancePaper: Paper: simulator learn.trade_evaluations — GET /v1/paper/trades/recent (same symbol as chart context; only closed rows with PnL count toward win/loss).
shadowLive.provenanceDivergence: Divergence table: decisions subset with match_ok=false or non-empty violation arrays — not a magic aggregate PnL delta.
shadowLive.provenanceAssessments: Deeper shadow-live reports: live.shadow_live_assessments and risk — per execution in the forensic timeline.
shadowLive.provenanceLineage: Market pipeline: GET /v1/live/state → data_lineage (Bitget/DB/Redis segments) — same market path as chart/signal, not automatically the same as the paper simulator.
shadowLive.summaryFootnote: Figures are window counts, not guaranteed KPIs. Paper rows without a close do not count as “closed”.
shadowLive.lineageLead: Live state for {symbol} / {timeframe} — if segments are empty, shadow/live comparison on the signal side is limited.
shadowLive.lineageUnavailable: Live state not loaded — no pipeline table.
shadowLive.lineageEmpty: No segments returned (cold start or lineage build issue).
shadowLive.divergenceLead: Fields from execution payload (shadow_live_divergence). A dash for shadow≈live means no value, not “ok”.
shadowLive.divergenceEmpty: No divergence rows in the current decision window.

signalsDetail.notFound: This signal was not found or you do not have access.
signalsDetail.summaryLead: The essentials in plain language — not investment advice.
signalsDetail.summaryDisclaimer: Signals are not investment advice. You make your own decisions; raw technical fields are in the collapsible operator section below.
signalsDetail.summaryDirection: System direction: {direction}. Strength {strength} of 100, estimated entry probability {probability}.
signalsDetail.summaryNoShortYet: No short note from the explain API yet — use the long form or Live AI below.
signalsDetail.summaryGlossary: Briefly: MAE = possible worst adverse path, MFE = possible best favourable path (both in basis points). OOD = unfamiliar market context for the model — interpret cautiously. Details and raw fields live in the technical section.

tradeActions.doNotTrade: No entry — system advised against a trade.
tradeActions.enterLong: Long entry possible from the system’s view — mind risk.
tradeActions.enterShort: Short entry possible from the system’s view — mind risk.
tradeActions.exitLong: Exit or reduce the long position.
tradeActions.exitShort: Exit or reduce the short position.
tradeActions.maybeEntry: Entry possible — proceed with caution and clear risk controls.
tradeActions.entry: Entry in the signal direction possible — mind risk.
tradeActions.noDirectionLongOrShort: No clear long/short direction — no entry.
tradeActions.directionMismatch: Direction does not fit context — no entry.

signalsDetail.sectionRiskLead: Warnings from the explain API, compact numbers and rationale from the signal row — without Live AI.
signalsDetail.sectionStoredLead: Texts from the database/explain API (short and long, engine list). Raw JSON only in the technical section.
signalsDetail.riskSnapshotLeverage: Leverage cap up to {allowed}×, system suggested {recommended}×.
signalsDetail.riskSnapshotStop: Stop distance roughly {dist} — orientation only, no fill guarantee.
signalsDetail.riskSnapshotOod: Model flags unfamiliar market conditions (OOD hint).
signalsDetail.explainLayer3LeadHuman: Compact list from the engine — see full JSON in the technical section.
signalsDetail.explainLayer3RawHint: Full reasons_json (raw) is below under “Technical details”.
signalsDetail.techToggleLead: Field names match API/persistence. Open only if needed — not required for the overview above.

signalsDetail.techGroupPolicyLead: Universal hard blocks apply hybrid; live-specific blockers may not stop paper/shadow.
signalsDetail.techStopAuditHint: Source: app.signals_v1 persistence (audit). Tight stops depend on tick size, spread and mechanics (fragility vs. executability).
signalsDetail.techPolicyBody: Hybrid trade_action follows universal hard blocks (e.g. uncertainty, exchange health). Account and portfolio stress via RISK_GOVERNOR_ACCOUNT_STRESS_LIVE_ONLY may block live execution only — paper/shadow can remain signal-active.
signalsDetail.aiStrategyLead: Only after the fixed layers above: separate LLM per explicit request. The snapshot is read-only; replies are freshly generated and are not automatically written to the database. No orders and no configuration changes.
signalsDetail.aiStrategySnapshotNote: The snapshot matches the gateway data loaded on this page. Optionally add a focus question (at least 3 characters) if you work without a snapshot.
signalsDetail.aiStrategyChartHint: If the response includes chart_annotations, the market chart below may show violet LLM overlays — technically bounded and tied to visible candles; no guarantee of 'perfect' lines.
signalsDetail.aiStrategyFocusPlaceholder: e.g. What risks do you see with this stop distance?
signalsDetail.aiStrategyErrNeedContextOrFocus: Without a signal snapshot, add a focus question with at least 3 characters.
signalsDetail.aiStrategyErrIncompleteResponse: The service returned data we could not interpret (strategy_explanation_de missing). Please retry.
signalsDetail.explanationLayersLead: Short definitions per signal API — does not replace the content above.
signalsDetail.explanationLayerLlm: Live AI only via separate BFF call
signalsDetail.explainStackLead: Read in order: short summary from the explain API, then stored long copy, then deterministic engine reasons, then optional live AI. None replaces the others.
signalsDetail.explainLayer1Lead: Short text from GET /v1/signals/{id}/explain (explain_short), from persistence/templates — not live AI.
signalsDetail.explainUnavailableBody: Stored explanation could not be loaded. Technical fields and live AI below may still work; short and long copy may be missing.
signalsDetail.explainLayer2Lead: Markdown from the explain API (explain_long_md). Fixed content from the signal-engine/gateway pipeline — not the same as live AI in step 4.
signalsDetail.explainLayer3Lead: reasons_json from the same explain response — authoritative engine audit JSON. Below that, a compact mirror derived from the signal row fields.
signalsDetail.rationaleNoTradeLead: Derived from flat signal-row fields — not LLM-invented.
signalsDetail.rationaleTradeLead: Only meaningful for the matching trade_action; otherwise empty.
signalsDetail.explainLayer4Aside: Step 4 is in the next panel: live AI only after you send a request.
signalsDetail.proposalDraftLead: Generates structured scenarios, parameter ideas, risk and validity notes, and optional chart annotations. Stored as a database draft — no order authority.
signalsDetail.proposalDraftBoundary: Promotion to paper, shadow, or live paths is only a logged intent after deterministic checks and explicit human acknowledgement. No broker or paper automation is started from this layer.
signalsDetail.proposalDraftFocusLabel: Optional focus question for the draft (German)
signalsDetail.proposalDraftFocusPlaceholder: e.g. Which scenarios do you see for a range break?
signalsDetail.proposalDraftTableMissingHint: Note: database table missing — run migration 615.
signalsDetail.proposalDraftHumanAck: I confirm this is not an automatic trade order; I will verify gates and runbooks separately.
signalsDetail.proposalDraftPromoFootnote: The server accepts promotion only after successful validation and with the acknowledgement checkbox set. Execution always goes through existing product and operations processes.

diagnostics.title: Central diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: All relevant checks in one place: connectivity, gateway, auth, broker, streams, data stores, event bus, reconcile, services, health/ready, cross-cutting incidents, and history—with filters, business priority, and export.
diagnostics.helpBrief: One action: full snapshot from gateway edge, system health, alerts and outbox—same data as self-healing, presented as a filterable matrix.
diagnostics.helpDetail: The table lists every registered component (SELF_HEALING_REGISTRY). Columns: business priority P1–P4, status, last collection time, human-readable finding, short technical trace as a cause hint, impact, recommended steps including automatic mitigations. Filters combine with AND. History is shared with the self-healing hub (session storage). Export: Markdown for tickets or ops.
diagnostics.summaryLead: Condensed view of the latest snapshot—no hunting across screens.
diagnostics.summaryCounts: Components: {ok} ok · {deg} degraded · {down} down · {inc} incidents total
diagnostics.summaryEdgeBlocks: Edge currently blocks v1 reads—check gateway bootstrap or configuration.
diagnostics.copyFailed: Clipboard unavailable—use download or select text manually.
diagnostics.filtersLead: Multiple filters apply together (AND). With none selected you see every check.
diagnostics.checksLead: Each row is a defined check—including services, databases, streams, broker, reconcile, and integrations.
diagnostics.causeOkShort: No deviation reported in this run.
diagnostics.emptyFiltered: No rows match the active filters.
diagnostics.crossLead: Items beyond a single component row—e.g. monitor alerts, health warnings, or missing health payload.
diagnostics.crossEmpty: No additional cross-cutting incidents in this run.
diagnostics.healingLead: What the platform is attempting or observing automatically—in addition to the table.
diagnostics.historyLead: Timestamps of collections and actions in this session.
diagnostics.historyShared: Same history as the self-healing hub (shared session storage).

selfHealing.title: Self Healing
selfHealing.subtitle: Central diagnostics and repair hub: full stack coverage, prioritized incidents, server-side refresh of health and edge probes, with clear boundaries for ops-only fixes.
selfHealing.toolbarLead: Each run combines gateway bootstrap (edge), GET /v1/system/health, open monitor alerts, and the alert outbox. “Full recheck” repeats those steps—without restarting workers in your cluster.
selfHealing.repairing: Server is re-running health and edge probes…
selfHealing.sectionHealing: What the platform is healing automatically
selfHealing.sectionManualLead: Items that need configuration, secrets, migrations, or human intervention—the UI can only re-measure and link you to the right surface.
selfHealing.sectionIncidentsLead: Deduplicated; severity reflects data path and execution impact.
selfHealing.sectionComponentsLead: Each row is a defined monitoring object (gateway health, edge, database, workers, integrations, freshness, operations).
selfHealing.sectionHistoryLead: Stored in this browser session (sessionStorage)—orientation only, not an audit log.
selfHealing.narrativeDisclaimer: No external LLM here: the explanation is derived from the snapshot. Use the operator assistant on the Health page for deeper root-cause work.
selfHealing.noneManual: No items that strictly require manual-only steps right now.

narrative.aggregateUnknown: Health aggregate: not reported or health not loaded.
narrative.aggregateGreen: Health aggregate: green—core paths look consistent from the gateway.
narrative.aggregateDegraded: Health aggregate: degraded—at least one path is only partially trusted.
narrative.aggregateRed: Health aggregate: critical—key prerequisites are missing or red.
narrative.counts: Components: {ok} ok, {degraded} degraded, {down} down, {nc} not configured. Incidents indexed: {inc}.
narrative.edgeBlocks: Edge diagnosis: authenticated /v1 reads are blocked—much of the console cannot refresh until JWT, gateway, or network are correct.
narrative.edgeOk: Edge diagnosis: the last bootstrap run did not block /v1 reads—if you still see red fields, inspect specific worker health.
narrative.noWorst: No degraded or down components in the last run.
narrative.footer: Note: worker restarts, docker compose, and secrets are not changed automatically—use runbooks and safe deploy paths.

healingHints.poll: We poll health and worker probes periodically via the API gateway—short blips are re-evaluated on the next cycle.
healingHints.bff: The dashboard BFF carries the operator JWT server-side only; on 401/403 re-mint JWT on the server.
healingHints.safeDegrade: On degraded responses the console shows read envelopes with hints and avoids risky partial fields.
healingHints.pathsOk: Last edge run: authenticated paths to the gateway were usable—remaining red rows usually mean specific workers or data lag.

causes.edgeOk: Bootstrap: path to the gateway looks OK from the dashboard server.
causes.bffBlocked: While the edge path is blocked, the BFF cannot refresh many /v1 datasets.
causes.bffOk: Edge is not blocking /v1 reads right now.
causes.noHealthPayload: Health response without the expected service list.
causes.noHealth: System health could not be loaded.
causes.dbOk: Postgres core connection OK per health.
causes.redisSkipped: Redis not checked or skipped here.
causes.migrationOk: Migrations fully applied or not reported.
causes.streamsIssue: At least one Redis stream reports an error or Redis is not OK (sample count: {n}).
causes.streamsOk: Redis stream sample shows no error rows.
causes.freshnessCandles: Candles considered stale or missing (health warnings).
causes.freshnessCandlesOk: No candle freshness warning in this run.
causes.freshnessSignalsOk: No signal freshness warning in this run.
causes.freshnessNewsOk: No news freshness warning in this run.
causes.noOpenAlerts: No open monitor alerts per counter.
causes.outboxBacklog: Outbox: high pending count ({pending})—check delivery path.
causes.outboxOk: Outbox shows no striking failure stack in this snapshot.
causes.integrationsMissing: Integrations matrix not present in health.
causes.integrationsOk: No red integration row in excerpt.
causes.providerOpsOk: Provider ops JSON shows no obvious failure pattern.
causes.providerOpsIssue: Provider ops suggests errors or degradation.
causes.httpBad: Gateway /health HTTP {code}—check gateway process or proxy.
causes.jwtOk: Operator JWT: last authenticated health path succeeded.
causes.jwtUnknown: JWT status ambiguous from last run.

impact.ok: {name}: no action needed for this run.
impact.notConfigured: {name}: not wired—HEALTH_URL or feature intentionally empty; no automatic healing.
impact.bad: {name}: impacts data quality, transparency, or execution until resolved.

impact.healthLoad: Without health, most console views cannot refresh.

executionTier.title: Execution posture
executionTier.healthUnreachable: Execution posture: the platform’s central health endpoint is not responding. Without it, execution posture is unreliable — open System & status and the connection diagnostic instead of guessing.
executionTier.tierMissing: Execution posture: health has no execution_tier (check gateway version).

api_gateway_url_missing.title: API gateway URL missing on the Next server
api_gateway_url_missing.body: Without API_GATEWAY_URL, /v1 data cannot load. In production this must be set explicitly (no silent localhost fallback).

dashboard_authorization_missing.title: Dashboard gateway auth missing
dashboard_authorization_missing.body: DASHBOARD_GATEWAY_AUTHORIZATION (Bearer JWT) is not set. Server components and the BFF need this token for GET /v1/*.

gateway_unreachable.title: API gateway unreachable
gateway_unreachable.body: Network error or timeout to GET /health. Common causes: stack not running, wrong API_GATEWAY_URL from the host, or firewall.

gateway_health_not_ok.title: Gateway /health reports an error
gateway_health_not_ok.body: The TCP connection works, but /health is not successful. Check api-gateway logs and process state.

gateway_not_ready.title: Gateway not ready (GET /ready)
gateway_not_ready.body: The gateway is up but reports ready=false — often bad HEALTH_URL_* inside the container (localhost instead of service DNS) or workers/DB/Redis down.

operator_jwt_unauthorized.title: Operator JWT invalid (HTTP 401)
operator_jwt_unauthorized.body: Signature, expiry, or audience mismatch. GATEWAY_JWT_SECRET on the gateway must match the mint; restart Next after minting.

operator_jwt_forbidden.title: Operator JWT forbidden (HTTP 403)
operator_jwt_forbidden.body: Token accepted but roles are insufficient (gateway:read). Check mint script and gateway roles.

operator_path_transport_failed.title: Authenticated gateway request fails on the network
operator_path_transport_failed.body: /health succeeded but GET /v1/system/health fails with a transport error — check proxy, URL, or an intermittent gateway crash.

operator_health_upstream_error.title: System health via gateway failed
operator_health_upstream_error.body: Auth is OK, but GET /v1/system/health returned an upstream error. Check gateway logs and worker readiness.

onboarding.metaDescription: Orientation: account, paper practice, chart, AI assistant and honest limits — no marketing promises.
onboarding.title: Welcome — your quick start
onboarding.subtitle: In a few clicks you know where to go for market view, paper practice, AI questions and system status — and what the app deliberately does not do. Open only what you need; skipping is always fine without losing features.
onboarding.footerNote: Reopen “Help & overview” and “Getting started” anytime from simple navigation under Help & guidance.
onboarding.errorSave: We could not save this step. Try again; if it persists, refresh the page.

start.title: You’re set to go
start.body: Your language is chosen. The interface follows — you can change it later in settings.
start.next: Optional: open language settings, or press Next for the account overview.

account.title: Understand your account
account.body: Profile, optional Telegram, balance and broker status live here. Sensitive data stays on the server — not in the browser.
account.next: Open “My account” for the overview; you don’t have to complete anything now.

demo.title: Paper practice or prepare the exchange
demo.body: Paper uses demo capital and simulator logic — no real exchange orders. Shadow and live are separate: shadow usually observes and compares alongside the decision chain; live moves real capital only with approvals. Broker connectivity is configured on the server — never with API keys in the browser.
demo.next: Open paper if you only want to practise; broker status when a real connection is being prepared.

chart.title: See your first chart
chart.body: The live terminal shows candles, the latest signal and whether market data is flowing — your first real market picture here.
chart.next: Open chart & market; if candles are missing, check system status later.

ai.title: First AI help
ai.body: Under “AI & system status” you ask in your chosen language. Answers are generated fresh and processed server-side — there is no long-lived chat transcript in this UI. AI summarises and explains; it does not grant silent trade approvals or change strategy parameters from the dialogue.
ai.next: Open the page and ask something concrete (e.g. what “live gate” means or why candles are empty).

safety.title: Safety and expectations
safety.body: Signals are system assessments — not buy advice and not a profit guarantee. AI and assistants are not investment advice. Paper protects you from real-money exposure; live stays deliberately restrictive. Cockpit and account show which paths are enabled for you.
safety.next: Optionally open cockpit and account — then finish to the overview.

consoleHome.kiPathTitle: Where AI helps in this app
consoleHome.kiPathBody: Under “AI & system status” the assistant answers in your language — fresh per request, no long-lived chat in this shell. Each signal detail page offers explanations and optional further AI steps; that is not investment advice. Model reports and drift sit under Pro: Learning & drift. Paper remains practice; live still needs approvals.

simple.title: Your start
simple.subtitle: Chart, signals, paper practice and AI questions — the rest of the console is under Pro.
simple.intro: Suggested order: Chart & market first (is data arriving?), then signals or paper. “AI & system status” combines health and the assistant — the latter explains but does not trade for you. “Help & overview” lists every entry without menu hunting. Signals are documented transparently — explicitly not buy advice.
simple.proHint: Cockpit, live broker, approvals, learning, market universe and more: switch to “All features (Pro)” at the top.

tileChart.title: Chart & market
tileChart.body: Candles, latest signal and data flow — see that data is arriving.

ai.title: AI & system status
ai.body: Assistant with a question field plus health overview — no automatic trades.

settings.body: Display language and more account preferences.

onb.title: Getting-started tour
onb.body: Guided steps returning to account — skippable anytime.

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.subtitle: What is waiting for approval — including Telegram status per execution.
approvals.contextHint: For cockpit and live broker see
approvals.apiErrorBody: We could not load the queue and Telegram status from the server. Nothing you approved is lost — open this page again in a moment.
approvals.empty: No open approvals in the queue.

public.flowNavAria: Navigation — home, product info and console
public.footerDisclaimer: Not investment advice. Metrics help you understand what is happening — no return guarantees. Live trading needs approvals and a secure server session.
public.metaDescription: Signals, approvals and practice mode. A calm, understandable interface.
public.heroLead: Signals, approvals and paper trading in one console. You decide what runs; we show what the system sees — without hype.
public.heroAudience: Built for people who operate or review a stack — not for anonymous one-click trading.
public.heroHint: Sign-in runs securely on the server — no exchange passwords in the browser.
public.svc1Body: Which instruments we follow and where practice or live is possible — without a hidden "everything goes" assumption.
public.svc2Body: Assessments with context you can follow — for information, not as a buy order.
public.svc3Body: See why the system sometimes pauses on purpose — without marketing spin.
public.svc4Body: Stops, leverage limits and budgets as guidance — not automatic risk tuning.
public.rel1Body: See what is waiting for you — including optional Telegram updates.
public.rel2Body: Understand decisions and timelines — for calm operations and follow-up questions.
public.rel3Body: How test runs differ from live — without false comfort.
public.driftLead: When models adapt, you see current state — approvals and limits stay on the server.
public.costLead: Plan, consumption and billing when your offering includes it — no hidden promises.
public.opsLead: Honest service status: what is up, what is failing, and what to check next.
public.adminBody: Advanced settings only with a valid server session. Sensitive keys never appear in the browser.
public.kiSectionLead: Models add context to signals and news, and aggregate drift reports. On Health you can ask a question in German; the answer comes from the server and is not saved as chat. Live orders still need your rules and approvals — nothing trades “by itself” from a text box.
public.kiStep1Body: Pick your language, optionally the short tour, then open the console. Without a running data stack, tiles may stay empty or show hints — that is normal, not a broken UI.
public.kiStep2Body: Under System & status you see whether market data, signals and services are reachable. AI-enriched content is reliable once those paths are healthy.
public.kiStep3Body: Signals and news: richer context. Learning & drift: aggregated model and drift reports from the platform. System & status: optional technical context plus the AI assistant for questions (no persistent chat, no trading authority).

usage.title: Usage & plan
usage.subtitle: Plan, consumption and billing — no ROI promise.
usage.dataHint: This view loads usage data only on the server (authenticated to the platform). It stays empty when billing is not active for this environment.
usage.err404: Billing is not active for this environment or your access does not include the usage API. Contact operations or support.

telegram.areaHint: Telegram alerts go through approvals and outbox — you can check status under approvals and in signal details.

commerce.customerAreaNote: Payment data is handled securely in the background; here you only see summaries.

adminRules.intro: No exchange or payment secrets in the browser. In production use NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_USE_SERVER_PROXY=true and keep DASHBOARD_GATEWAY_AUTHORIZATION server-side only (full Authorization header with JWT).
adminRules.proxyHint: No token in the browser — authentication runs in the Next.js server.
adminRules.tokenPlaceholder: Admin token (local only, when not using server proxy)

strategyStatus.disabledGateway: Not enabled: missing or invalid server-to-gateway authentication. Check NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_USE_SERVER_PROXY and DASHBOARD_GATEWAY_AUTHORIZATION.
strategyStatus.errNoToken: No admin token: set one under Admin first.
strategyStatus.hintProxy: Authentication uses the Next.js server — no token in the browser.
strategyStatus.hintToken: A valid token is required (session, set under Admin).

ui.refreshHint: We will retry automatically on the next page load or when you refresh. Live views also poll or use real-time updates once the connection is back.

llmAnswer.authorityBody: This model explains and frames information — it does not place orders, change risk or system settings, or replace human review.
llmAnswer.authorityNone: According to the server response, the AI is not allowed to approve trades or releases.
llmAnswer.authorityUnusual: The server reports a non-standard role for this answer — if unclear, check logs or ask your team.
llmAnswer.sectionNext: Sensible checks you can do next
llmAnswer.nextStepsOperator:
If something stays vague: ask more specifically or add JSON context from health or signals.
For technical terms: use your runbooks or architecture docs — the assistant is not operator training.
Before real decisions: review system health, data flow, and approvals as you usually do.
llmAnswer.nextStepsStrategy:
Compare the explanation with the numbers in the overview and stop-budget — the model does not automatically see everything.
To go deeper: use the optional focus question and send again.
Trading, approvals, and risk gates still follow your fixed paths, not this text box.
llmAnswer.metaCachedYes: Answer served from cache (same input as before)

executionPath.unavailable: No execution data from the server.

healthSnapshot.refreshHint: Check network and gateway, then reload. The top console ribbon also indicates when health is unreachable.

incident.cascadeHint: Sections below show a short pointer only; full context is here — and in diagnostic mode if needed.
incident.priorityBlocker: Priority: action needed — until fixed, console data stays incomplete or blocked.
incident.priorityDegraded: Priority: degraded — often transient; per-area details below.
incident.secondaryTitle: Other data sources with a different issue
incident.modulePending: {module}: not loaded — see the central notice above.

stateActions.groupAria: Next steps for connection or loading issues

appError.title: This view could not be loaded
appError.body: Something went wrong on the technical side. Please reload the page. If it keeps happening, open System & status and check platform connectivity.

notFound.refreshHint: Check the address or return to the list — the item may be missing, deleted, or not visible for your account.

degraded.title: Limited data
degraded.refreshHint: The table may still be usable — review the notice, check health or connectivity, then reload later.

assist.emptyTitle: No reply in this conversation yet
assist.emptyBody: Ask a question for the selected tab and send it. The assistant only uses the JSON context you optionally provide — no automatic trades or approvals.
assist.emptyRefreshHint: If something fails: shorten the input, fix JSON, or reload; check connectivity on Health.

chart.errorGeneric: Could not load the chart. Check connectivity and reload the page.
chart.emptyCandles: No candles yet for this symbol and timeframe — check the pipeline or try again later.
chart.badgeExecutionHint: Configured execution mode (paper/shadow/live) from system health or broker runtime.
chart.badgeFreshnessHint: Freshness of Bitget/pipeline market data for this timeframe.
chart.prefsSaveFailed: Could not save symbol or timeframe on the server — navigation still works, but your last choice may not restore on the next visit.
chart.openLiveTerminal: Open live terminal with the same symbol
chart.llmLayerLead: Violet lines and markers come from the language model and are separate from the rule-based strategy overlay below. Not a trading approval. Visible candles bound what is drawn — geometry far outside plausible ranges is dropped.
chart.llmLayerFootnote: No claim of complete or permanently correct rendering: model interpretation, technically filtered.
chart.llmLayerMsNormalized: Note: {count} timestamp(s) were adjusted from milliseconds to seconds (candle axis).
chart.llmLayerGeometryFiltered: Note: geometry from the model sat outside the visible candle window — only text notes are shown.

strategy.invalidationHint: Backend fields trade_action, decision_state, regime_transition_state — no extra prices invented in the browser.

strategy.hintStop: Stop (from stop_distance_pct): {price} — backend stop_distance_pct={pct} (fraction of price).
strategy.hintStopNoPct: Stop line: {price} (backend stop_distance_pct missing or invalid).
