layerHint.reference: Reference price from market data (mark, ticker, or last close).
layerHint.risk_mae: Price from expected_mae_bps and direction (adverse move).

unreachable.title: Service temporarily unreachable
unreachable.body: We could not reach the platform right now — for example a short restart or network issue. Your data is not lost; please try again in a moment.

timeout.title: Request timed out
timeout.body: The request took too long. Please wait a moment and reload the page.

unauthorized.title: Sign-in required
unauthorized.body: These data need a valid session or your sign-in has expired. Please sign in again or contact your provider’s support.

forbidden.title: Access denied
forbidden.body: Your account is not allowed to view this resource.

not_found.title: Not found
not_found.body: This content does not exist (anymore) or your access does not cover it.

bad_gateway.title: Service temporarily impaired
bad_gateway.body: Part of the platform is not responding as expected. This is usually temporary — please try again later.

server_error.title: Server error
server_error.body: Something went wrong on the server side. Please try again later.

validation.title: Input not accepted
validation.body: The selection or input was not accepted. Please check filters and fields.

parse.title: Unreadable response
parse.body: The response was not in the expected format. Try reloading the page.

bff_unreachable.title: Dashboard connection interrupted
bff_unreachable.body: The link between the browser and the API proxy failed. Restarting the Next.js app often helps.

configuration.title: Backend setup incomplete
configuration.body: The dashboard server is not fully configured to reach the API gateway — this is an operations issue, not your browser. Please verify server configuration or contact whoever runs the environment.

unknown.title: Could not load data
unknown.body: Something unexpected happened. Reload the page or check Health to see if the stack is running.

errors.generic: Something went wrong on our side. Try refreshing the page. If it keeps happening, wait a minute and try again — under System & status you can see whether the platform is reachable.
errors.unsupportedLocale: This language is not available in the app yet. Pick another language in the bar above.
errors.invalidJson: We could not process that input. Check the format and try again.
errors.fallbackMessage: We could not reach the platform right now. Check your connection and try again in a moment.
errors.loadDataPrefix: We could not load this data:
errors.facetsFallback: Filter statistics are temporarily unavailable. You can still change filters; counts may catch up after a reload.

gateway.summaryDegraded: The platform is returning this data in a limited or stale form — not everything on screen is fully up to date.
gateway.summaryWithReason: Server context: {reason}. The UI therefore shows guidance and only safe partial fields where needed.
gateway.summaryEmptyState: There are no rows for this filter or time range — either raw data is missing or the pipeline is intentionally empty.
gateway.impactDegraded: Do not base risk, approvals, or execution decisions solely on this partial view — check status and health.
gateway.impactEmpty: You intentionally see an empty list; this is not a connectivity error unless another alert is red.
gateway.urgencyDegraded: Medium — you can work, but use extra caution until data is complete again.
gateway.urgencyHint: Low — informational unless another warning is active.
gateway.appDoing: We keep reading safe endpoints, keep the session stable, and only show validated partial fields when necessary.
gateway.userActionDefault: Wait briefly, then use “Reload this view”. If it persists: open System & status or contact operations.

unreachable.headline: The platform is not responding right now
unreachable.summary: We could not complete a round-trip between your browser and our servers — often right after a deploy, during network issues, or while the stack is still starting.
unreachable.impact: This page cannot show current market or account data; approvals and risk views may be incomplete.
unreachable.urgency: High while it lasts — do not treat this as a live decision surface.
unreachable.appDoing: The browser does not retry forever; after a reload we attempt a clean connection again.
unreachable.userAction: 1) “Reload this view” or reload the whole page. 2) Open System & status to see whether services are healthy. 3) If it continues, contact support or operations with the time and screen.

timeout.headline: The request took too long
timeout.summary: The server or an intermediate service did not answer in time — common under load or with slow queries.
timeout.impact: This tile’s data is missing; other areas may still work.
timeout.urgency: Medium — a single timeout is often harmless.
timeout.appDoing: We cancel hung requests so the UI does not freeze.
timeout.userAction: Reload or try again later. If timeouts cluster, ask operations to check load and the database.

unauthorized.headline: Your sign-in is missing, expired, or not valid here
unauthorized.summary: This data is only available to signed-in, authorised accounts — your token is absent, expired, or mismatched to the environment.
unauthorized.impact: No operational data and no order or approval actions until the session is valid.
unauthorized.urgency: Blocking for this function until sign-in is fixed.
unauthorized.appDoing: We do not echo secrets in the error; the session is not silently “repaired”.
unauthorized.userAction: Sign in again or renew the dashboard server JWT (operations: DASHBOARD_GATEWAY_AUTHORIZATION / mint script). Do not confuse with the internal service key.

forbidden.headline: Access denied
forbidden.summary: You are signed in, but your account is not allowed to see this resource with the current roles.
forbidden.impact: Data and actions for this area stay hidden — this is intentional protection.
forbidden.appDoing: No automatic escalation; your permissions are not changed silently.
forbidden.userAction: If you need access, ask an administrator or operations for the right role.

not_found.headline: This content is not here
not_found.summary: The path or record was not found — deleted, never existed, or the ID belongs to another context.
not_found.impact: Display and actions would target nothing real; further steps would be misleading.
not_found.urgency: Low to medium — depends on whether you expected an active item.
not_found.appDoing: We return a clear error instead of silent placeholder data.
not_found.userAction: Check the URL or selection, go back one step, or refresh the list.

bad_gateway.headline: A service in the chain is not responding correctly
bad_gateway.summary: The API gateway or a worker behind it reported an intermediate error (e.g. 502/503) — often brief during restarts or when a peer is not ready.
bad_gateway.impact: Live or reference data may be incomplete; automated decisions on this basis are unreliable.
bad_gateway.urgency: High until health is green again.
bad_gateway.appDoing: Server-side retries and circuit breakers limit damage; the UI shows the state clearly.
bad_gateway.userAction: Open System & status and the connection diagnostic; if it persists, notify operations with a timestamp.

server_error.headline: Unexpected server error
server_error.summary: The platform is reachable, but something internal failed while processing.
server_error.impact: The requested operation did not complete reliably; the view may be incomplete.
server_error.urgency: Medium to high — repeated 5xx should be escalated.
server_error.appDoing: Errors are logged; sensitive content is not shown in the UI.
server_error.userAction: Do not blindly repeat the action; wait, reload, then contact support with a correlation ID if shown.

validation.headline: Invalid input or filters
validation.summary: The server rejected the parameter combination — e.g. invalid range or missing required fields.
validation.impact: No partially wrong data is mixed in; the request fails clearly.
validation.appDoing: We reject the request instead of silent correction so conclusions stay trustworthy.
validation.userAction: Review filters and inputs, simplify values, and try again.

parse.headline: The response could not be read
parse.summary: The payload was not valid JSON or was corrupted — proxy, cache, or a faulty gateway template may be involved.
parse.impact: This data tile cannot be filled; others may still work.
parse.appDoing: We do not show synthetic substitute data that could mislead.
parse.userAction: Reload, enable diagnostic mode to inspect the raw response, and notify operations if reproducible.

bff_unreachable.headline: The protected app bridge is not responding
bff_unreachable.summary: The Next.js server could not reach the internal proxy (/api/dashboard/...) or it crashed.
bff_unreachable.impact: Browser and API gateway are not safely coupled — no secure operator data in the client.
bff_unreachable.appDoing: The browser does not silently call the gateway directly (no JWT in the client).
bff_unreachable.userAction: Restart the dev server; in production check deployment/pod logs and dashboard server environment variables.

configuration.headline: Server configuration is incomplete
configuration.summary: The dashboard server is missing required settings (e.g. API_GATEWAY_URL or valid DASHBOARD_GATEWAY_AUTHORIZATION) for a safe platform attachment.
configuration.impact: No reliable market, risk, or broker data — the UI stays in a deliberate safe failure mode.
configuration.appDoing: We do not start unsafe fallbacks with fabricated data.
configuration.userAction: Operations/admin: check .env or the secret store, mint a new JWT, redeploy. End users: contact support.

unknown.headline: Unexpected error while loading
unknown.summary: Something failed without a clear classification — details may appear under technical details.
unknown.impact: This view is not trustworthy until the error is understood.
unknown.appDoing: Errors are logged; there are no silent background retries without your reload.
unknown.userAction: Reload, enable diagnostics, contact support with time and page URL.

terminal.chartsNoCandles: No candles yet: data may still be loading, or symbol/timeframe does not match what the platform stores. Check “Where does market data come from?” below.
terminal.btnReloadAria: Reload market data from the server now
terminal.chartCaption: Candles from platform history. Freshness and execution lane are in the live data summary above; expand technical details if you need them.
terminal.techDetailsSummary: Technical details (history store, buffer, market freshness)
terminal.reconnectHint: When the connection is back, we refresh the chart and side panel automatically (real-time or short polling).
terminal.errAuthP1: This view needs a server-side link to the platform. Operations: verify dashboard server environment variables (
terminal.errAuthP3: ). Restart the dev server after changes, then reload.
terminal.errProxy: Market data is loaded through a protected app proxy. If this persists: start the stack, verify connectivity, restart the app. Technical diagnostics:
terminal.errDirect: Check that the platform at {browserUrl} is reachable and CORS allows your origin. For details: stack check / operations docs.
terminal.freshnessLive: current — prices are updating as expected
terminal.freshnessDelayed: slightly delayed — last candle or transfer older than usual
terminal.freshnessStale: out of date — feed or history lags badly; do not treat as live
terminal.freshnessDead: very old — no recent candles or prices; chart for orientation only
terminal.freshnessNoCandles: no candles yet for this symbol and interval
terminal.freshnessUnknownTf: interval not recognised for freshness checks
terminal.streamSseOff: real-time off on server — polling every {seconds}s
terminal.transportHintSseOff: Real-time is disabled on the server — we fetch on a short interval (every {seconds}s). Ask operations to enable streaming if you need continuous updates.
terminal.transportHintSseGaveUp: The live connection failed — we keep refreshing every {seconds}s. Reload the page or contact operations if this persists.
terminal.transportHintPolling: Until real-time is active, this view refreshes every {seconds}s.
terminal.demoDataBody: This view may show placeholders from optional SQL demo seeds, fixture files, or the Bitget demo API — not necessarily a full live pipeline.

demoReason.schema_migration_demo_trace: Database: demo/legacy migration recorded in app.schema_migrations

lineage.title: Where does market data come from?
lineage.lead: Each row is one step from the exchange feed to this chart. Where it says “missing”, signals or extras are often missing too.

platformLineage.title: Execution mode & data streams
platformLineage.lead: From the latest health snapshot: which lanes are active, last candle and signal times, market-stream and live-broker status, and reconcile — same view as on Market universe.
platformLineage.gridAria: Execution mode, LIVE/SHADOW/PAPER lanes, stream and broker health

paperPanel.title: Paper positions
paperPanel.empty: No open paper positions. The paper broker reacts to signals; without running engines the list stays empty — entries appear once the pipeline delivers data.

signalPanel.emptyBody: No signal for this symbol and timeframe — check the signal engine, data freshness, or filters.

newsPanel.title: News
newsPanel.empty: No news in this snapshot. When the news pipeline or gateway provides items, they appear here — otherwise check the symbol or demo data.

dataSituation.title: Live data posture
dataSituation.subtitle: Execution lane, market/feed reality, last ingest age, pipeline completeness — no pretending live when it is not.
dataSituation.laneHint: Paper/shadow/live describes execution configuration, not every dataset automatically.
dataSituation.dataBadgeHint: Market or feed reality for this surface (candles, API lists, health snapshot…).

source.signalsApi: API gateway GET /v1/signals/recent (signal list — not a candle live feed)
source.brokerApi: Live-broker APIs (runtime, orders, fills — not a candle stream)

dataSituation.completenessNa: not computed for this surface (no lineage)
dataSituation.shadowPartialSections: Shadow/live comparison: {n} API section(s) did not load fully — some areas may be empty or unreliable.
dataSituation.hintDemoFixture: Note: demo/fixture paths are active — the UI does not claim a full live exchange path.
dataSituation.hintDegradedGeneric: Gateway reports degraded reads — fewer fields or older snapshots possible.
dataSituation.healthOverviewLead: Platform candle/signal timestamps from health — not the same as the chart symbol.
dataSituation.muMetaLead: Market overview: catalog snapshot below; candle live applies only to the selected chart symbol.
dataSituation.signalsFeedLead: The table shows filtered API signals — empty can mean filters or a dry pipeline, not your browser.
dataSituation.brokerLead: Broker page: {sections} API sections failed — some cards may be empty or stale.

common.safetyAiLead: Prefilled context includes technical hints for this surface. Answers are indicative — see prompt 38 architecture and error dossier 08.
common.embeddedSafetyLead: Embedded safety diagnosis: adjust the question and submit. Context is pre-seeded for this surface.
common.sectionCauses: Likely causes (priority depends on your stack)

consoleChartFetchFailed.title: Console market chart: fetch failed
consoleChartFetchFailed.lead: The browser could not load live state for chart candles. Without a successful fetch the chart stays empty or only shows the chart widget error.
consoleChartFetchFailed.cause1: Dashboard BFF or gateway unreachable (network, TLS, wrong base URL).
consoleChartFetchFailed.cause3: Upstream Python/gateway returns 5xx or times out — check body and x-request-id.
consoleChartFetchFailed.cause4: Symbol/timeframe rejected server-side or empty body despite 200.
consoleChartFetchFailed.services: Next.js dashboard, gateway HTTP, Python live-state services, optional Redis buffer.
consoleChartFetchFailed.interfaces: Client fetchLiveState → BFF live-state route; dashboard API routes; gateway aggregation.
consoleChartFetchFailed.step1: Browser network tab: failed request, status, response text.
consoleChartFetchFailed.step2: Reload with ?diagnostic=1; compare live terminal page.
consoleChartFetchFailed.step3: stack:check / gateway logs; health page if reachable.
consoleChartFetchFailed.step4: After fix: hard reload; for 401/403 check session/cookies.
consoleChartFetchFailed.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Why is console market chart live-state/candles fetch failing? Please outline gateway/BFF/data pipeline suspects and which logs to open first.

consoleChartEmpty.title: Console market chart: no candles
consoleChartEmpty.lead: The request succeeded but the candle list is empty — usually data or configuration, not just UI.
consoleChartEmpty.cause1: No aggregated candles yet for this symbol/timeframe.
consoleChartEmpty.cause2: Market ingest/stream pipeline stopped or lags heavily.
consoleChartEmpty.cause3: Demo/fixture mode deliberately sparse for the instrument.
consoleChartEmpty.cause4: Gateway normalises symbol differently — note “resolved” under the chart.
consoleChartEmpty.services: Market ingest, candle DB aggregation, Redis buffer, Bitget REST/WebSocket per profile.
consoleChartEmpty.interfaces: Live-state endpoint; apps/dashboard fetchLiveState; backend candle tables and workers.
consoleChartEmpty.step1: Try a more liquid symbol and a shorter timeframe.
consoleChartEmpty.step2: Open live terminal: lineage + freshness badges.
consoleChartEmpty.step3: Read health execution mode, demo hints, service list.
consoleChartEmpty.step4: Ops: ingest logs and last successful candle timestamp in DB.
consoleChartEmpty.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Console chart returns 200 but no candles. Which pipeline stages (ingest, DB, gateway) should I check first per dossier 08?

consoleChartFreshness.title: Console market chart: poor or stale market data
consoleChartFreshness.lead: Candles render but freshness reports stale, dead, or no_candles. Treat trading/risk decisions accordingly.
consoleChartFreshness.cause1: Ticker or candle ingest stopped or heavily delayed.
consoleChartFreshness.cause2: Redis live buffer or stream consumer impaired.
consoleChartFreshness.cause4: Host clock skew inflates lag metrics.
consoleChartFreshness.services: Market stream, Redis, gateway freshness calc, Bitget REST.
consoleChartFreshness.step1: Use freshness badge and terminal diagnostic numbers.
consoleChartFreshness.step2: Check Redis and stream processes on the stack.
consoleChartFreshness.step3: Compare external last price vs API ticker.
consoleChartFreshness.step4: Document maintenance windows; otherwise open an incident.
consoleChartFreshness.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Console shows bad market freshness despite visible candles. Name the most likely technical cause and services to verify per our error architecture.

terminalFetchFailed.title: Live terminal: state fetch failed
terminalFetchFailed.lead: HTTP reload or initial fetch for terminal state failed. Chart and side panels may be incomplete.
terminalFetchFailed.cause1: Same class as console chart fetch (network, auth, 5xx).
terminalFetchFailed.cause2: SSE does not replace first REST fetch — isolate REST first.
terminalFetchFailed.cause3: Very large payload or gateway timeout.
terminalFetchFailed.cause4: Local dev without valid operator env for dashboard server.
terminalFetchFailed.services: Dashboard BFF, gateway live state, DB/Redis per health.db/redis in state.
terminalFetchFailed.interfaces: fetchLiveState in terminal client; live terminal route.
terminalFetchFailed.step1: Capture banner message and raw text with ?diagnostic=1.
terminalFetchFailed.step3: Check edge-status JSON and gateway reachability.
terminalFetchFailed.step4: Reload after fix; SSE may reconnect automatically.
terminalFetchFailed.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Live terminal fetchLiveState fails. Provide a prioritized checklist across gateway, DB, and auth.

terminalStreamStale.title: Live terminal: realtime stream quiet (stale)
terminalStreamStale.lead: No server ping on SSE for a long time. UI falls back to HTTP polling and may look stale.
terminalStreamStale.cause1: Gateway LIVE_SSE_ENABLED=false or SSE breaks upstream.
terminalStreamStale.cause2: Redis pub/sub or event source stopped emitting.
terminalStreamStale.cause3: Client network drops persistently (proxy, VPN, sleep).
terminalStreamStale.services: Gateway SSE, Redis, live event worker, proxy timeouts.
terminalStreamStale.step1: Read stream badge and transport hints.
terminalStreamStale.step2: Gateway logs for SSE disconnects and ping intervals.
terminalStreamStale.step4: Increase proxy read_timeout or accept polling until fixed.
terminalStreamStale.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Terminal SSE is stale: which component should I check first (gateway, Redis, network) and which symptoms confirm it?

terminalEmptyCandles.title: Live terminal: no candles loaded
terminalEmptyCandles.lead: Terminal state has zero candles. Lineage and freshness narrow the gap.
terminalEmptyCandles.cause1: No successful backfill after start/reconnect yet.
terminalEmptyCandles.services: History DB, ingest, gateway aggregation, demo seeds.
terminalEmptyCandles.step1: Read empty-state copy and lineage OK/total.
terminalEmptyCandles.step3: Cross-check health and ops at same time.
terminalEmptyCandles.step4: Ops: SQL last candle for symbol.
terminalEmptyCandles.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Terminal shows 0 candles while parts of health look OK — which lineage segment per dataflow dossier first?

terminalFreshnessBad.title: Live terminal: market freshness critical
terminalFreshnessBad.lead: Freshness is stale, dead, or no_candles — similar to console but with terminal lineage context.
terminalFreshnessBad.cause2: Ticker dead while candles partial or vice versa.
terminalFreshnessBad.cause4: Symbol not wired to stream in market universe.
terminalFreshnessBad.interfaces: state.market_freshness; freshness banner in terminal client.
terminalFreshnessBad.step1: Read banner and optional diagnostic lines.
terminalFreshnessBad.step2: Compare with console chart for same symbol.
terminalFreshnessBad.step3: Check stream and REST in parallel.
terminalFreshnessBad.step4: Scan alert outbox for repeated market alerts.
terminalFreshnessBad.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Terminal freshness is critical: hypotheses tying Redis, stream, and DB?

healthPageLoadFailed.title: Health page: aggregate load failed
healthPageLoadFailed.lead: At least one parallel fetch (system health, open alerts, outbox) failed. The page may be partial.
healthPageLoadFailed.cause2: Dashboard server cannot resolve operator secrets.
healthPageLoadFailed.cause4: Partial denial via rate limit or WAF.
healthPageLoadFailed.services: Dashboard SSR fetch, gateway health aggregator, monitor for alerts.
healthPageLoadFailed.step1: Read error text and dashboard server logs.
healthPageLoadFailed.step4: After recovery reload; safety AI below includes dashboard_load_error in JSON.
healthPageLoadFailed.suggestedSafetyQuestion: Health page throws while loading. Most likely cause among BFF, gateway, monitor — and concrete log locations?
healthPageLoadFailed.safetyBelowHint: The safety diagnosis panel below already includes dashboard_load_error in context — run the AI there instead of duplicating inline.

operatorExplainLlmFailed.title: Operator explain AI: request failed
operatorExplainLlmFailed.lead: operator-explain call failed or returned no valid result. No extra judgement on live trading.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.cause2: Budget, rate limit, or model unavailable.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.cause3: BFF validation: context JSON too large or invalid.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.services: Dashboard /api/dashboard/llm/operator-explain, Python LLM orchestration, provider API.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.step1: Copy error text and trace IDs if shown.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.step2: Retry with short question and small context.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.step3: Health: AI usage and provider status.
operatorExplainLlmFailed.suggestedSafetyQuestion: operator-explain failed. Likely causes (provider, timeout, validation) and the next two debug steps?

openAlertsEscalated.title: Monitor: escalated open alerts
openAlertsEscalated.lead: At least one open alert has high or critical severity — treat as immediate review, not background noise.
openAlertsEscalated.cause1: Automatic rule repeatedly failed (data gap, order path).
openAlertsEscalated.cause3: Dependent service still in error state.
openAlertsEscalated.services: Monitor engine, alert outbox/delivery, domain services per alert title.
openAlertsEscalated.interfaces: Monitor alerts API; health table; ops runbooks.
openAlertsEscalated.step2: Open related health warnings and integrations matrix.
openAlertsEscalated.step3: Check outbox delivery status and last errors.
openAlertsEscalated.step4: Close or acknowledge alerts per ops process after fix.
openAlertsEscalated.suggestedSafetyQuestion: We have escalated open monitor alerts. Prioritised root-cause hypothesis and which service logs to open first?
